title: Codex provider parity audit and test hardening
description:
Goal: bring the OpenAI/Codex-compatible provider path up to the same reliability level as the Anthropic path.

Scope:
- Investigate current provider compatibility for the OpenAI/Codex-compatible provider in this repo
- Audit existing unit + integration test coverage for the OpenAI-compatible / Responses API path
- Add missing test infrastructure parity: integration framework support comparable to Anthropic's mock API, cache validation, prefix validation, and realistic restart/resume validation
- Add whatever tests and fixes are needed so the Responses API path has the same level of support and confidence as Anthropic

Constraints:
- This should be handled as a single parent task that decomposes itself into sub tasks as needed
- The executing agent should act as a sub-orchestrator: first investigate architecture + test gaps, then create focused sub tasks, then merge and validate
- Follow test-is-golden strictly; for any bug fix discovered, write the failing test first
- Work across the relevant provider, test-utils/mock API, integration tests, and any shared provider-loop code as needed

Why:
The user feels the Codex/OpenAI path may lag behind Anthropic in compatibility and testing rigor. We want honest parity, not marketing parity: comparable framework support, validation, restart behavior, and confidence from tests.
